title: We Built A Sam Neill Personality Quiz Because Honestly, No Actor Has Ever Ranged This Hard
dek: From dinosaur-whisperer to literal Antichrist to Hobbit-forest grump — answer honestly and we'll tell you which Sam Neill role is basically your personality on screen.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just go with your gut and we'll match you to the Sam Neill role hiding inside you. Take the quiz, then open Flickle to find out where to actually watch your match tonight.

questions.question: Something completely impossible just happened right in front of you. What's your first move?

answers.text: Stand up slowly, take off my sunglasses, and just stare in total awe
Smile calmly — I'm already three steps ahead figuring out how this benefits me
Get obsessed. I need to understand it even if it destroys me
Insist there's a rational explanation, loudly, to anyone who'll listen

questions.question: How would your friends describe your leadership style?

answers.text: Charismatic and terrifyingly persuasive
Brilliant but a little too willing to cross lines for the mission
Confident right up until reality proves me completely wrong
Gruff, low-key, but you'd trust me in a crisis

questions.question: A kid you barely know gets dumped on you to take care of. Honest reaction?

answers.text: I'd probably forget they exist mid-experiment
I'd assume they're lying to me about something
Grudging acceptance that somehow turns into real love
I'd take it as a sacred responsibility
A small isolated town nobody's heard of — what could go wrong
The middle of the bush with no phone signal
Anywhere with a good story and someone who needs saving
A remote colonial outpost where I can control everything

questions.question: Someone challenges your worldview directly to your face. What happens?

answers.text: I don't engage. I just do my own thing
I listen, weigh it fairly, then act on my conscience
I shut down and get quietly furious
I double down and moralize even harder

questions.question: What's your biggest personality flaw, if we're being honest?

answers.text: I meddle in things that aren't my business, for 'the greater good'
I'm emotionally repressed to a genuinely destructive degree
I confuse my own ego with moral superiority
I get so wrapped up in wonder I forget the danger

questions.question: Someone you trusted completely just betrayed you. How do you react?

answers.text: It boils over into something I can't take back
I feel personally, righteously wronged
Shock first, then quiet, wounded disappointment
Cold, calculated retaliation — no drama needed

questions.question: Pick the soundtrack that plays over your life:

answers.text: Brooding period-crime brass, very self-serious
Sweeping orchestral score, all wonder and awe
Smooth, ominous strings — something's coming and it's me
Dissonant, unsettling ambient noise
Showing you something amazing and watching your face light up
Grooming you into my perfect protégé (concerning, but it's love)
Sharing an obsession, even a dangerous one
Sarcasm that's secretly deep affection

questions.question: Last one — what's your ideal way to spend a Friday night?

answers.text: A black-tie event where I'm quietly the most powerful person in the room
Alone in a lab, chasing an idea way past midnight
Debating conspiracy theories just to prove everyone wrong
Campfire, no plans, good company, dead quiet

results.name: Dr. Alan Grant
results.subtitle: Jurassic Park (1993)
results.description: You're equal parts wonder and skepticism — the person who gets genuinely giddy about something incredible right before your survival instincts kick back in. You didn't ask to be a father figure, but somehow you end up being exactly that when it counts.

results.name: Damien Thorn
results.subtitle: Omen III: The Final Conflict (1981)
results.description: Calm, charming, and quietly terrifying — you get what you want by being smooth about it, not loud. People trust you right up until it's way too late to matter.

results.name: Dr. William Weir
results.subtitle: Event Horizon (1997)
results.description: You're brilliant, obsessive, and a little too willing to follow an idea straight off a cliff. Nobody watches you slide into the dark all at once — it happens so gradually that even you don't notice until it's done.

results.name: John Trent
results.subtitle: In the Mouth of Madness (1994)
results.description: Professional skepticism is basically your entire personality — you assume everyone's running a con and every ghost story is a cheap trick. The problem is when you're finally, catastrophically wrong.

results.name: Uncle Hec
results.subtitle: Hunt for the Wilderpeople (2016)
results.description: Gruff on the surface, grieving underneath, and way more tender than you'll ever admit out loud. You don't do easy laughs or easy feelings — you earn both the hard way.

results.name: Merlin
results.subtitle: Merlin (1998)
results.description: Humble, honorable, and quietly carrying the weight of everyone else's fate on your shoulders. You love hard, fight for what's right, and somehow never lose your decency, even across decades of betrayal.

results.name: Alisdair Stewart
results.subtitle: The Piano (1993)
results.description: Rigid, repressed, and bound by rules you didn't choose but can't seem to break free of. You're not a villain by nature — you're just someone whose frustration eventually has nowhere left to go but out.

results.name: Chief Inspector Chester Campbell
results.subtitle: Peaky Blinders (TV)
results.description: You're absolutely convinced you're the good guy, and that conviction is exactly what makes you dangerous. Self-righteous monologues are basically your love language.
